title: String references should be avoided
description: String references should not be usedAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityreliabilityCode SmellreactWhy is this an issue?More InfoReact Refs provide a way to access DOM nodes or React elements created in the render method.Older React versions allowed the ref attribute to be a string, like"textInput", later accessible asthis.refs.textInput. This is considered legacy code due to multiple reasons:Stringrefsmake React slower as they force React to keep track of what component is currently executing.Stringrefsare not composable: if a library puts a ref on the passed child, the user can’t put another ref on it.The owner of a stringrefis determined by the currently executing component.const Hello = createReactClass({ componentDidMount() { const component = this.refs.hello; // No
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

checks.message: React string refs are deprecated. Use React.createRef() or useRef() hook instead.
React string refs are deprecated. Use ref callbacks or React.createRef() instead.
